assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and local machine.
No Pulumi Cloud account, CLI login, cloud provider credentials, project, or stack update exists yet.

developer_goal: Create Pulumi access and deploy the first infrastructure resource for one declared provider and language route.

first_success.outcome: For the representative AWS Get Started candidate, the stack update creates the tutorial bucket and AWS CLI lists it without error.
first_success.observable_signal: aws s3 ls s3://$(pulumi stack output bucketName) returns an empty listing without error.

route_selection.rule: Do not invent a winner among peer clouds and languages on the Get Started router; leave the route unresolved until product, provider, language, and state backend are fixed.

candidates.name: Pulumi IaC with AWS and TypeScript S3 tutorial
candidates.reason: A common documented deploy-and-verify path, but Get Started requires choosing among AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Kubernetes and then a language.

candidates.name: Pulumi IaC with Azure, Google Cloud, or Kubernetes
candidates.reason: Peer provider tutorials with different accounts, credentials, resources, and verification commands.

candidates.name: Pulumi Cloud signup identity method
candidates.reason: Public signup offers GitHub, Google, SSO, and work email peers; required email fields and post-create CLI login prompts are not fully fixed from public evidence alone.

required_path.action: Create a Pulumi Cloud account from the signup surface.

required_fields.label: Signup identity method
required_fields.notes: Public UI offers Continue with GitHub, Google, SSO, and work email.

required_fields.label: Work email
required_fields.notes: Visible for the email continuation path; OAuth routes may not collect it on this page.

required_fields.label: Terms of Service and Privacy Policy acknowledgement

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a Pulumi Cloud identity that can authenticate the CLI or console.

required_path.action: Install Pulumi CLI and language runtime prerequisites for the selected tutorial.

required_fields.label: Language runtime prerequisites
required_fields.notes: AWS Get Started lists different runtimes per language tab.

required_path.observable_result: pulumi version runs and the chosen language toolchain is available.

required_path.action: Authenticate Pulumi to a state backend and configure cloud provider credentials.

required_fields.label: Pulumi login or backend authentication
required_fields.notes: Get Started assumes access configuration; exact CLI login prompts depend on backend choice.

required_fields.label: Cloud provider credentials
required_fields.notes: AWS account is a documented prerequisite for the AWS tutorial.

required_path.observable_result: Pulumi and the cloud provider CLI can act on the target account.

required_path.action: Create the tutorial project and stack, run pulumi up, and verify the resource.

required_fields.label: pulumi new prompts
required_fields.notes: Exact new-project prompts depend on template and language.

required_fields.label: pulumi up confirmation
required_fields.notes: Deploy docs require approving the update.

required_path.observable_result: The stack update finishes and the verification command lists the new bucket without error.

prerequisites.description: An AWS account and local AWS CLI access for the representative AWS verification command.

external_gates.description: Cloud provider credentials and permissions must allow creating the tutorial resource.

unavoidable_waits.description: pulumi up must create the cloud resource before verification can succeed.

platform_outcomes.description: Pulumi creates the stack resources in the target cloud and writes stack outputs such as bucketName.

excluded.item: Reading Pulumi concepts and migration guides
excluded.reason: Supporting documentation, not required product actions for first deploy.
Peer provider route, not selected until the router choice is fixed.

sources.title: Get Started with Pulumi
evidence_supported: Provider router for AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Kubernetes

sources.title: Pulumi Cloud signup
evidence_supported: GitHub, Google, SSO, and work email signup options

sources.title: Kubernetes Get Started configure
evidence_supported: Kubernetes as a peer Get Started provider

uncertainties.question: Which Pulumi product, cloud provider, language, and state backend define first success?
uncertainties.impact: These choices change account work, install prerequisites, credentials, prompts, and the verification command.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded goal naming provider and language, plus a fresh Pulumi Cloud signup and CLI walkthrough through the tutorial verification command.
